channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Sourcery vs SonarQube: Refactoring vs Analysis
item.description: Quick verdict Sourcery and SonarQube solve different parts of the code quality problem - and understanding that difference is what makes this comparison useful. Sourcery is an AI code review and...

item.title: Odoo vs. NetSuite vs. SAP: Which ERP Actually Fits a Mid-Market Business?
item.description: At some point in the life of a growing business, someone in a leadership meeting says the words “we need an ERP” and the room nods collectively, as if everyone knows exactly what that means and...

item.title: I Crashed My Mac 5 Times So You Don't Have To: Mounting S3 Files on macOS
item.description: Two days ago, AWS launched S3 Files — a managed NFS layer that turns any S3 bucket into a mountable filesystem. Sub-millisecond latency within AWS. Full read/write. Bidirectional sync. The AWS...

item.title: next-intl vs next-i18next: Choosing the Right i18n Library for Next.js
item.description: Next.js developers choosing an i18n solution will likely narrow it down to two libraries: next-intl and next-i18next. Both support the App Router and Server Components. Both are actively...

item.title: Run Leetcode locally - Javascript test case for practices
item.description: Leetcode debugging feature sits behind paywall. Stop paying for that! I once wish i could just use VScode and leverage some of the debugging feature you can do that easily, but how about the test...

item.title: Resilient Retries: The API Tactics That Shrink Tail Latency
item.description: The counterintuitive math of duplicate requests — when sending 2x traffic actually reduces server load Resilient Retries: The API Tactics That Shrink Tail Latency The counterintuitive math of...

item.title: Inside Anthropic's Project Glasswing: The AI Model That Found Zero-Days in Every Major OS
item.description: Inside Project Glasswing: The AI Model That Found Zero-Days in Every Major OS On April 7, 2026, Anthropic announced something that most cybersecurity professionals have been dreading: an AI model...

item.title: SoloEngine: Low-Code Agentic AI Development Platform with Native Support for Multi-Agent Collaboration, MCP, and Skill System

item.title: RxJS in Angular — Chapter 6 | Error Handling — Building Apps That Don't Break
item.description: "Error Handling — Building Apps That Don't Break" 👋 Welcome to Chapter 6! You've built beautiful features using Observables. But what happens when the internet goes down? What if the API server...

item.title: Building MovieSync in Public: A Real-Time Dashboard for Smarter Movie Intermissions
item.description: Building MovieSync in Public (with Antigravity) 🎬🚀 Building in public means sharing not just the outcome, but the tools and decisions behind it. While creating MovieSync, I also leveraged...

item.title: OpenAI Backs Bill That Would Limit Liability for AI-Enabled Mass Deaths or Financial Disasters
item.description: The ChatGPT-maker testified in favor of an Illinois bill that would limit when AI labs can be held liable—even in cases where their products cause “critical harm.”

item.title: ChatGPT has a new $100 per month Pro subscription
item.description: OpenAI has announced a new version of its ChatGPT Pro subscription that costs $100 per month. The new Pro tier offers "5x more" usage of its Codex coding tool than the $20 per month Plus...

item.title: Here are the best Apple Watch deals available right now
item.description: In September, Apple launched its latest batch of smartwatches, including the Apple Watch Series 11, the SE 3, and the Ultra 3. Each has its own pros and cons, but the introduction of Apple’s...

item.title: Florida launches investigation into OpenAI
item.description: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier is launching an investigation into OpenAI over public safety and national security risks, as reported earlier by Reuters. In a statement on Thursday,...

item.title: The EFF is quitting X
item.description: The digital privacy non-profit Electronic Frontier Foundation will no longer be posting on X as of Thursday, largely due to a sharp decline in views on the platform over the past several years. In...

item.title: ChatGPT finally offers $100/month Pro plan
item.description: OpenAI announced on Thursday something that power users have been asking for: a $100/month plan. Previously, subscriptions jumped from $20 to $200 per month.

item.title: John Deere will pay farmers $99 million over right-to-repair lawsuit
item.description: John Deere has agreed to pay farmers $99 million to resolve a class action lawsuit that accused the agricultural giant of preventing farmers and mechanics from accessing the materials needed to...

item.title: Samsung’s Galaxy Watch 8 is easier to recommend now it starts at $260
item.description: Samsung’s Galaxy Watch 8 is one of the best Android smartwatches you can buy right now, especially if you’re already using a Samsung phone. Right now the 40mm, Bluetooth-enabled version’s on sale...

item.title: Florida AG announces investigation into OpenAI over shooting that allegedly involved ChatGPT
item.description: ChatGPT had reportedly been used to plan the attack that killed two and injured five at Florida State University last April. The family of one victim has said that they plan to sue OpenAI over the...

item.title: NASA’s Artemis II mission to fly around the far side of the Moon
item.description: Humans haven’t set foot on the Moon since NASA’s Apollo 17 mission in 1972. Now, the space agency is racing to get back to the lunar surface under the umbrella of its Artemis program — a nod to...

item.title: “Negative” views of Broadcom driving thousands of VMware migrations, rival says
item.description: Western Union exec says there were "challenges" working with Broadcom.

item.title: After data breach, $10B-valued startup Mercor is having a month
item.description: After falling victim to a hacker, Mercor is facing lawsuits and reportedly losing big-name customers.

item.title: Instagram now lets you edit your own comments
item.description: If you make a mistake in an Instagram comment, you can now finally fix it without having to delete the comment and make it again. On Thursday, Instagram announced that users can now edit their...

item.title: Meta AI app climbs to No. 5 on the App Store after Muse Spark launch
item.description: The app was ranking No. 57 on the App Store just before Meta AI's new model launched. Now it's No. 5 — and rising.

item.title: Is Anthropic limiting the release of Mythos to protect the internet — or Anthropic?
item.description: Anthropic said this week that it limited the release of its newest model, dubbed Mythos, because it is too capable of finding security exploits in software relied upon by users around the world....

item.title: Google and Intel deepen AI infrastructure partnership
item.description: The two tech giants are looking to co-develop custom chips, at a time when demand for CPUs is high due to a growing global shortage.

item.title: Netflix’s TV games get a big boost with Jackbox collection
item.description: Netflix's party-focused lineup of TV games just got three excellent new additions: Jackbox's Drawful 2, Fibbage 4, and Quiplash 3, available as the Jackbox Party Essentials pack on the platform....

item.title: The 70-Person AI Image Startup Taking on Silicon Valley's Giants
item.description: Black Forest Labs has long punched above its weight in the AI image generation space. Its next move? Powering physical AI.

item.title: EngageLab SDK Flaw Exposed 50M Android Users, Including 30M Crypto Wallet Installs
item.description: Details have emerged about a now-patched security vulnerability in a widely used third-party Android software development kit (SDK) called EngageLab SDK that could have put millions of...

item.title: Sierra’s Bret Taylor says the era of clicking buttons is over
item.description: Last month, Sierra launched Ghostwriter, an agent designed to build other agents. With this “agent as a service” tool, the startup intends to replace traditional click-based web applications with...
